name: Command Execution in Critical Namespaces by Non-System User
notes:
This rule detects execution of a command in a critical namespace, for example kube-system in Kerbernetes, by a non-system user. Normal users should not interact with system resources.

Note: Edit the rule to replace "system:serviceaccount" by typical Service Accounts on the system
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefiniton: Containers
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Procedure/Trigger Execution
when the event matches Username is not any of system:serviceaccount
when the event matches Namespace (custom) is any of [kube-system or kube-public]
TD_list: APPLY Command Execution in Critical Namespaces by Non-System User on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefiniton: Containers
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Procedure/Trigger Execution
AND when the event matches Username is not any of system:serviceaccount
AND when the event matches Namespace (custom) is any of [kube-system or kube-public]
RED_list: Event name: Command Execution in Critical Namespaces by Non-System User
Event description: Detects when a command execution in critical namespaces by a non-system user.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
